evals.prompt: Audit this dataset for FAIR compliance: a CSV file with three columns — 'Sample ID', 'Compressive Strength (MPa)', 'Water Absorption (%)' — for 10 geopolymer concrete samples. No README, no metadata, no data dictionary, and no repository link are provided.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must audit all four FAIR dimensions: Findable (no DOI, no metadata record, no repository — flag as not findable), Accessible (CSV is local-only, no access protocol — flag as not accessible), Interoperable (no standard vocabulary, no schema, no units specification — flag as not interoperable), Reusable (no README, no license, no provenance, no contact — flag as not reusable). The output must not skip any dimension.

assertions.name: findable_audited
assertions.description: The Findable dimension is checked: DOI, metadata, repository.

assertions.name: accessible_audited
assertions.description: The Accessible dimension is checked: access protocol, authentication, persistence.

assertions.name: interoperable_audited
assertions.description: The Interoperable dimension is checked: standard vocabulary, schema, units, format.

assertions.name: reusable_audited
assertions.description: The Reusable dimension is checked: README, license, provenance, contact.

evals.prompt: Generate a data availability statement for a paper on 'Microstructure and mechanical properties of ZrO2-toughened alumina ceramics'. The raw data (XRD patterns, SEM images, mechanical test data) will be deposited in Zenodo with embargo until publication.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must detect the domain as 'ceramics' and generate a data availability statement that includes: the repository name (Zenodo), the embargo period, the types of data (XRD, SEM, mechanical tests), and the access conditions. The statement must not claim the data is 'available upon request' if it is going to a repository, and must not claim 'publicly available' before the embargo lifts. The statement must follow the conventions in references/data-availability-statements.md.

assertions.name: domain_detected
assertions.description: The assistant identifies the domain as ceramics.

assertions.name: repository_named
assertions.description: The statement names the specific repository (Zenodo).

assertions.name: embargo_stated
assertions.description: The statement includes the embargo period and access conditions.

assertions.name: no_false_public_claim
assertions.description: The statement does not claim 'publicly available' before the embargo lifts.

evals.prompt: Validate this CSV against the asphalt data schema: columns are 'Sample', 'Temp (°C)', 'Penetration (dmm)', 'Softening Point (°C)', 'Ductility (cm)'. The data contains 8 rows of asphalt binder test results.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must validate the CSV columns against the asphalt data schema (references/asphalt-data-schema.md). It should check: (1) required columns are present, (2) column naming conventions match the schema, (3) units are specified in headers, (4) data types are appropriate. If any column is missing or misnamed, the assistant should report the discrepancy. The output must reference the asphalt-data-schema.md file.

assertions.name: schema_referenced
assertions.description: The assistant references asphalt-data-schema.md or the appropriate domain schema.

assertions.name: columns_checked
assertions.description: The response checks all columns against the schema for presence, naming, and units.

assertions.name: discrepancies_reported
assertions.description: Any missing or misnamed columns are reported with specific recommendations.

evals.prompt: Create a dataset README for a thermal insulation materials dataset. The dataset includes: thermal conductivity at 25 °C, 50 °C, 100 °C for 5 aerogel composite samples; density; porosity by mercury intrusion; SEM images of pore structure. The user provides the contact email and measurement methods.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must include all required fields from the dataset README template (assets/templates/dataset-readme-template.md): materials description (aerogel composites, sample IDs), methods (thermal conductivity by guarded hot plate, porosity by MIP, SEM), variables (temperature, thermal conductivity, density, porosity), units, and contact information. The output must not omit any required field.

assertions.name: materials_described
assertions.description: The README includes a materials description with sample IDs.

assertions.name: methods_described
assertions.description: The README includes measurement methods (guarded hot plate, MIP, SEM).

assertions.name: variables_and_units
assertions.description: The README lists all variables with their units.

assertions.name: contact_included
assertions.description: The README includes contact information.

evals.prompt: Convert this experiment-record.yaml to a structured dataset package. The experiment record describes a cement mortar study with 3 mix designs, each tested for compressive strength at 7 and 28 days with 3 replicates, and setting time. The record includes raw data file paths, test standards (ASTM C109, ASTM C191), and the researcher's contact.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must convert the experiment record into a dataset package following references/experiment-record-to-dataset.md. The output should include: (1) a structured CSV data file with columns for mix ID, age, replicate, compressive strength, setting time, (2) a dataset README with methods, variables, units, standards, and contact, (3) a metadata file. The conversion must preserve all quantitative data, replicate counts, and test standards from the original record.

assertions.name: csv_structure_correct
assertions.description: The CSV includes columns for mix ID, age, replicate, compressive strength, and setting time.

assertions.name: readme_generated
assertions.description: A dataset README is generated with methods, standards, variables, units, and contact.

assertions.name: standards_preserved
assertions.description: ASTM C109 and ASTM C191 are preserved in the output.

assertions.name: replicates_preserved
assertions.description: The 3 replicates are preserved in the structured data.

evals.prompt: Create an asphalt FAIR dataset package from an experiment-record.yaml supplied by materials-doe, with a later request for a data availability statement.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should load the minimal data core first, then load the experiment-record conversion rules, asphalt schema, and availability rules only for the relevant output. It must preserve validation-before-scaffolding, the data-package handoff contract, and visible missing-data boundaries without treating shared stance, ethics, or the legacy duplicate contract as always-load content.

assertions.name: shared_rules_are_on_demand
assertions.description: The default router keeps operating stance, ethics, and the legacy data-contract entry outside always_load.

assertions.name: doe_and_domain_routes_are_conditional
assertions.description: Experiment-record validation and conversion load only for DOE-backed input, while asphalt schema and availability rules load only for their relevant tasks.

assertions.name: data_package_contract_preserved
assertions.description: The output retains raw versus processed separation, explicit units and replicate counts, validation-before-scaffolding, and the data-package handoff fields.
